goal: Reconcile the missing BP-149 anchor documents for the historical /probe/sandbox-write implementation that existed before BP-150 retired it. Record that BP-149 replaced the fixed permit demo trigger with an env-gated, caller-path sandbox writer against G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox, including optional delete/undo behavior, and explicitly mark that route as later retired by BP-150.
scope_out: Do not reintroduce /probe/sandbox-write code or tests.
Do not change the BP-150 retired route.
Do not touch SmartPush, the Maya gate client, permit registers, providers, package files, or tools.
Do not perform a live GitHub write.
Do not implement the Codex-as-Executor path in this reconciliation.
claims: BP-149 existed as historical work in the pre-BP-150 Bluepilot tree, not merely as a stray STATE.md sentence.
The historical route was env-gated by BLUEPILOT_SANDBOX_PERMIT_WRITE_ENABLED but did not require a permitId/writePermit/corridor consume.
The historical route targeted only G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox on main, accepted a validated path plus contentBase64 for writes, and accepted op:'delete' for undo/delete.
BP-150 later retired the route with HTTP 410 and removed the content write/delete body.
This reconciliation changes documentation only and does not reopen the retired route.
assumptions: origin/main is at or after ba19c33, where BP-150 has already retired /probe/sandbox-write.
The repository history available locally is shallow/grafted; c7c3c97 is the evidence-bearing pre-BP-150 snapshot for BP-149 behavior.
The missing BP-149 contract and review packet are an anchor-doc drift, not a reason to re-create the retired implementation.
dod: contracts/BP-149.json exists and records BP-149 as historical documentation, not an active route contract.
review-packets/BP-149.md exists and records the git evidence, actual historical route behavior, and BP-150 retirement.
STATE.md and docs/SESSION-LOG.md no longer understate the optional delete/undo behavior from c7c3c97.
No builder code, test, route, runtime flag, or package file changes.
stop_conditions: Any BP-150 route behavior change becomes necessary.
Any live write is requested as proof.
Any executor/autonomy wiring change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BP-148 defused earlier legacy write paths. BP-149 then introduced an env-gated but permitless /probe/sandbox-write door for Maya sandbox writes and undo/delete metadata. BP-150 later retired that side door and removed its write/delete body. This BP-149 document is a retrospective reconciliation of missing anchor artifacts.
